compiled_state.title: [create-dso-app]: test stub passes plugin_root as arg2 but production code never reads it
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
Source: code review (passed) — minor finding not addressed in this session.
Finding: The test stub dso-setup.sh receives two positional args (project_dir and plugin_root) but only uses $1. The production code passes both via bash "$_setup_script" "$project_dir" "$resolved_plugin_root". The stub and real script should agree on which args are used.
File: tests/scripts/test-create-dso-app.sh
Category: verification

comments.body: Investigation (INTERMEDIATE, opus): Ticket premise is incorrect — production dso-setup.sh DOES read $2 as PLUGIN_ROOT at line 102. create-dso-app.sh:439 correctly passes resolved_plugin_root. The test STUB at tests/scripts/test-create-dso-app.sh:1110-1120 ignores $2, which is an acceptable test-double pattern (stub only needs to simulate shim creation). A legitimate coverage gap exists: no test asserts create-dso-app.sh passes plugin_root as arg2. ESCALATE: user decision needed — (a) close as not-a-bug, (b) strengthen test stub to record arg2 and add assertion.
comments.author: Test
